samples.hypothesis_text: Bonjour, je veux tester une phrase longue sans faire de pause pour voir si Parakeet V3 coupes des mots à la fin quand je parle longtemps de manière continue.
Je vais dicter rapidement cette phrase pour vérifier si le modèle suit correctement quand le débit de parole augmente et que les mots arrivent presque sans interruption.
Je parle volontairement avec une voix plus basse pour vérifier si le modèle comprend encore correctement le mot, même quand le volume du micro est plus. faible

Today I want to run a real long form dictation test to see whether the model can keep the full meaning and without losing the final part when I continue speaking for much longer than in the short test we used before.

This test is meant to sound more natural than a simple scripted sentence because I want to speak the way a normal user will speak while working and thinking at the same time and I want to know whether the transcript still stays readable., accurate and stable when the rhythm is not perfectly even and the idea develops over claws.

I am going to explain a product issue. a realistic way as if I were talking to someone on the team during a work session and the important thing here is not only whether the app catches the right words but also whether it keeps the structure of the explanation clear enough to read afterwards because a user may dictate a long message like this for one or two minutes without true stopping.

I want to test how the app behaves when I speak with natural pauses because in real life people do not usually they stop for a second to think they take a breath, they restart the sentence. and then they continue so this test should help us see whether those pose create strange puncture cuts or missing pieces in the final transcript.

Right now I am doing a longer speaking test with pauses in unusual places because something sometimes user hesitate in the middle of a thought and then continue after a short silence. And what I want to check is whether the app still keeps the whole sentence correct coherently, instead of splitting it badly adding too many periods or dropping the words that come right after the pause.

Yeah and the correction in a sensible way.

At first I was going to say that we should ship the update on Friday. Actually, no let me rephrase that I thought. And I want to know if the transcript handles this kind of spoken correction cleanly without creating duplicated fragments or strange filter word.

Okay, so imagine I am explaining something casually to a teammate and I am not trying to read in a polished voice. I am just speaking the way I normally would and that the app works pretty well most of the time but when the recording becomes longer and the speech is more spontaneous. The real question is whether the transcript still feels like something a person would actually want to copy paste into a message document or note.

This is another long conversational sample and the goal is to see whether the model keeps up when the speaker changed direction a little inside the same message because a lot of people start with the one idea then add context and then come back to the original point and what matters for user experience is that the final text still makes sense without looking broken.

For this test I am going to vary the rhythm on purpose so sometimes I will speak fairly quickly and then I will be able to do it. slow down and then I will pause for a moment before continuing. Because I want to stress the chunking and ask some logic and they see whether the app begins to lose word duplicated illite sections or insert punctuation that does not really match the way a human would write the same idea.

The reason I am doing this recording is that short benchmark sentences can look very good while real user still feels and what I really care about now is whether the transcript stays stable over time while I continue talking. changing speed, changing tone and sometimes pausing briefly because that is much closer to

I am now doing a longer dictation that should last closer to 2 minutes and I want to use it as a stress test for the full experience because in real life someone may open the app and speak for a long stress while writing notes after a meeting or while thinking through the problem out loud and if the module start well but slowly shift add the wrong punctuation changes language or begins to miss the endings of sentences then the experience will still feel unreliable even if the short test look good.

The final English long form sample is here to test endurance more than anything else and what I want to know is whether the up remains consistent from the first sentence to the last one because sometimes transcription system looks accurate for ten or twenty seconds and then becomes much weaker once the message with more pauses, more breath, more hesitation and more natural spoken structure to this is the kind of recording that should reveal those problems clearly.

Je termine avec une dictée française plus longue pour voir si l'application reste stable sur une durée plus importante. parce que the very problem ne se vopa toujours sort of phrase court and terse is nous intéresse maintenant, c'est de savoir si au bout de trente secondes, une minute ou presque deux minutes, la transcription continue de suivre correctement. passer soudainement en anglais, sans perdre des mots importants et sans transformer la structure de la phrase en quelque chose de difficile à relire.
